explanations.3: This tells PHP to enforce strict type checking, so if you pass the wrong type of value to a function, it will error instead of trying to convert it.
explanations.13: This syntax uses a named argument to tell Rector to apply modernizations for PHP 8.2, the latest version of the language.
explanations.14: These are predefined rule sets that Rector will apply: codeQuality improves how the code is written, deadCode removes unused stuff, and typeDeclarations adds type hints where they're missing.
